pattern: $state and $derived runes for reactive Apple tokens
implementation: <script> let isDark = $state(false); let bgColor = $derived(isDark ? '#000000' : '#fbfbfd'); let textColor = $derived(isDark ? '#f5f5f7' : '#1d1d1f'); </script>
anti_pattern: Svelte 4 stores for simple local reactive state in Svelte 5 components
notes: Runes are Svelte 5's compile-time reactive primitives with zero overhead

pattern: CSS custom properties for Apple design tokens
implementation: <style> :global(:root) { --color-text-primary: #1d1d1f; --color-text-secondary: #86868b; --color-bg: #fbfbfd; --color-blue: #2997ff; --space-xs: 4px; --space-sm: 8px; --space-md: 16px; --space-lg: 24px; --space-xl: 48px; } </style>
anti_pattern: Hardcoded pixel values in component styles
notes: Global CSS token definitions in root layout give a single source of truth

pattern: transition:spring for physics-based animations
implementation:
content
// Or with spring store: const pos = spring({ y: 0 } { stiffness: 0.3 damping: 0.8 });
anti_pattern: CSS ease-in-out for interactive element transitions
notes: Svelte's spring primitive matches UIKit's spring physics for natural motion feel

pattern: animate:flip for list reordering with spring physics
implementation:
{#each items as item (item.id)}
{item.name}
{/each}
anti_pattern: Abrupt DOM reordering without animated transitions
notes: flip calculates FLIP animation automatically matching UIKit animated data source updates

pattern: {#if} blocks for progressive disclosure patterns
implementation:
{#if showDetails}
{@render details()}
{/if}
anti_pattern: Showing all content at once or using CSS visibility hacks
notes: Mirrors Apple's progressive disclosure pattern hiding complexity behind user-initiated reveals

pattern: use:action for intersection observer scroll reveals
implementation: function intersect(node onEnter) { const obs = new IntersectionObserver(([e]) => { if (e.isIntersecting) { onEnter(node); obs.disconnect(); } } { threshold: 0.1 }); obs.observe(node); return { destroy() { obs.disconnect(); } }; } // Usage: <section use:intersect={reveal}>
anti_pattern: Scroll event listener for visibility detection
notes: Actions keep DOM logic encapsulated and reusable matching Apple's component model

pattern: svelte:window for system theme detection
implementation: <svelte:window on:change={handleMediaChange} /> <script> import { browser } from '$app/environment'; let isDark = $state(browser && matchMedia('(prefers-color-scheme: dark)').matches); if (browser) { matchMedia('(prefers-color-scheme: dark)').addEventListener('change' e => { isDark = e.matches; }); } </script>
anti_pattern: Polling document for theme or JS-only toggle
notes: Reactive OS-level dark mode detection without polling or flicker

pattern: $effect for dark mode class toggling
implementation: $effect(() => { document.documentElement.classList.toggle('dark' isDark); document.documentElement.setAttribute('data-theme' isDark ? 'dark' : 'light'); });
anti_pattern: Manually calling DOM methods outside reactive context
notes: Svelte $effect runs after state changes keeping DOM in sync with reactive dark mode state

pattern: Component composition matching Apple component hierarchy
implementation: <!-- Page.svelte --> <Header /> <main> <HeroSection /> <FeatureGrid {features} /> <CTASection /> </main> <Footer /> <!-- Each section is a self-contained Svelte component -->
anti_pattern: Single monolithic page component with all markup inline
notes: Mirroring Apple's view hierarchy keeps each component focused and testable

pattern: -apple-system font stack in app.css
implementation: body { font-family: -apple-system BlinkMacSystemFont 'Inter' 'Segoe UI' system-ui sans-serif; -webkit-font-smoothing: antialiased; -moz-osx-font-smoothing: grayscale; }
anti_pattern: Component-scoped font-family overrides
notes: Global font stack in app.css ensures consistent SF Pro / Inter rendering across all routes

pattern: Backdrop-filter in Nav component
implementation: <nav class='nav'> <slot /> </nav> <style> .nav { backdrop-filter: saturate(180%) blur(20px); background: rgba(255 255 255 / 0.72); border-bottom: 1px solid rgba(0 0 0 / 0.1); position: sticky; top: 0; z-index: 100; } </style>
anti_pattern: Opaque solid background on sticky navigation
notes: Apple's frosted glass nav signature across all platforms

pattern: Apple spacing scale as Svelte CSS variables
implementation: :global(:root) { --space-xs: 4px; --space-sm: 8px; --space-md: 16px; --space-lg: 24px; --space-xl: 32px; --space-2xl: 48px; }
anti_pattern: Arbitrary margin values inside each component
notes: 8pt-based spacing scale ensures visual rhythm matching Apple's grid

pattern: 44px minimum touch targets
implementation: button a [role='button'] { min-height: 44px; min-width: 44px; touch-action: manipulation; display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; }
anti_pattern: Small icon buttons without padding
notes: Meets Apple HIG tap target minimum; touch-action removes 300ms click delay

pattern: prefers-reduced-motion in Svelte transitions
implementation: <script> import { prefersReducedMotion } from '$lib/utils'; const duration = prefersReducedMotion ? 0 : 450; </script> <div transition:fly={{ y: 20 duration }}>
anti_pattern: No animation preference handling
notes: Respects iOS Reduce Motion for users with motion sensitivity

pattern: SvelteKit layout for consistent Apple nav/footer
implementation: <!-- src/routes/+layout.svelte --> <script> import TheHeader from '$lib/TheHeader.svelte'; import TheFooter from '$lib/TheFooter.svelte'; </script> <TheHeader /> <slot /> <TheFooter />
anti_pattern: Repeating header/footer in every route component
notes: Root layout persists between navigations without remounting matching Apple nav persistence

pattern: SvelteKit loading with Apple skeleton pattern
implementation:
{#each { length: 6 } as _}
{/each}
anti_pattern: Spinner-only or no loading state
notes: Content silhouettes at correct dimensions prevent layout shift matching Apple's skeleton pattern

pattern: SvelteKit form actions for Apple-style form handling
implementation: <!-- +page.server.ts --> export const actions = { default: async ({ request }) => { const data = await request.formData(); // process... return { success: true }; } }; <!-- +page.svelte --> <form method='POST' use:enhance>
anti_pattern: Client-side fetch in onMount for form submission
notes: Progressive enhancement via form actions works without JS matching Apple's reliability standard

pattern: bind:this for focus management and accessibility
implementation: <script> let dialogEl = $state(); function openDialog() { showDialog = true; tick().then(() => dialogEl?.focus()); } </script> <dialog bind:this={dialogEl} role='dialog'>
anti_pattern: No programmatic focus management on modal open
notes: Correct focus management required for VoiceOver and keyboard accessibility

pattern: Spring store from svelte/motion for interactive animations
implementation: <script> import { spring } from 'svelte/motion'; const scale = spring(1 { stiffness: 0.4 damping: 0.7 }); </script> <button onmouseenter={() => $scale = 1.05} onmouseleave={() => $scale = 1} style='transform: scale({$scale})'>
anti_pattern: CSS transform with ease-in-out on hover
notes: Spring store gives physically plausible hover animations matching UIKit spring behavior

pattern: Tweened store for smooth value transitions
implementation: <script> import { tweened } from 'svelte/motion'; import { cubicOut } from 'svelte/easing'; const progress = tweened(0 { duration: 600 easing: cubicOut }); </script> <progress value={$progress} max={100} />
anti_pattern: Abrupt value jumps in progress indicators or counters
notes: Tweened values animate smoothly without manual requestAnimationFrame matching Apple's polished feel
